picks.rationale: The Philadelphia Phillies are heavily favored, with 88% of moneyline bets and 80% of run-line bets on them, which often indicates a public overreaction and potential value on the underdog. [5] Despite Phillies starter Jesús Luzardo's strong recent form (4-0, 2.28 ERA in his last seven starts), the Mets just took the series opener. [5, 13] The Mets have also covered the run line in 6 of their last 10 games and are on a two-game ATS winning streak, suggesting they can keep games close even against a favored opponent. [25]

Astros pitcher Spencer Arrighetti is projected to allow over 4.5 hits, with a projected probability of 52-53% compared to the implied probability of 45.45% at +120 odds, indicating strong value. [15] Arrighetti has a history of giving up hits, having allowed five or more in several recent outings against various teams. [15] The Baltimore Orioles are currently on a five-game winning streak, suggesting their offense is in good form and capable of capitalizing on Arrighetti's tendencies. [24, 37] Arrighetti's fastball vulnerability and walk rate often lead to traffic on the bases, increasing opportunities for hits. [15]

picks.game: Los Angeles Dodgers @ New York Yankees
picks.rationale: Dodgers pitcher Emmet Sheehan is a strong candidate for over 5.5 strikeouts, with a projected probability of 55-57% compared to the implied probability of 50.25% at -101 odds. [6] The New York Yankees are significantly hampered by key offensive injuries, including Aaron Judge (stress fracture) and Giancarlo Stanton, which could lead to more strikeout opportunities for opposing pitchers. [27] This favorable matchup against a weakened Yankees lineup, combined with the value in the odds, makes this a high-confidence prop bet. [6]

picks.game: Tampa Bay Rays @ Boston Red Sox
picks.rationale: The Boston Red Sox are currently on an impressive 11-game winning streak, demonstrating exceptional recent form. [39] They have been highly profitable for bettors, hitting the moneyline in 16 of their last 18 games with a 61% ROI. [30] Playing at home, the Red Sox hold a significant advantage, and their starting pitcher, Patrick Sandoval, boasts a solid 2.08 ERA. [30] While the Rays have a good overall record, the Red Sox's current momentum and home-field performance make them a strong pick at these odds.

The St. Louis Cardinals defeated the Arizona Diamondbacks 5-4 in the previous game, ending Arizona's four-game winning streak. [9, 45] The Diamondbacks are dealing with several key injuries, including starting pitcher Zac Gallen and outfielder Tommy Troy, which could impact their performance. [2, 27] Despite Merrill Kelly's (ARI) recent strong outings, the Diamondbacks' defense committed errors leading to unearned runs in the last game, indicating potential vulnerabilities. [45, 46] At even money, the Cardinals present a valuable opportunity given their recent win and Arizona's injury concerns.

Third-place playoff matches historically tend to be more open and high-scoring, as the pressure of the final is off and teams often rotate or play with more attacking freedom. Both Kylian Mbappe for France and Harry Kane and Jude Bellingham for England are actively chasing the Golden Boot, providing strong individual motivation for goals. Head-to-head history between these two nations shows an average of 2.78 goals per game in their last nine meetings, with 67% of their last three encounters going over 2.5 goals. Furthermore, England has seen both teams score in all four of their knockout matches in this tournament, indicating a propensity for high-scoring games.

France enters this match with superior recent form, boasting a 2.50 points-per-game average over their last six matches compared to England's 1.83. England is significantly impacted by injuries and fatigue, with key midfielder Jordan Henderson definitively ruled out due to a broken wrist, Declan Rice expected to be rested due to ongoing fitness issues, and Reece James doubtful after an injury in the semi-final. In their recent head-to-head encounters, France holds a slight edge with five wins to England's three and one draw in nine recorded meetings. The market also favors France, indicating a belief in their stronger position for this bronze medal match.
